title: Pictures From Theocritus - From Idyl I.
content:
[Greek: Ady ti to psthyrisma], etc.

    Goat-herd, how sweet above the lucid spring
    The high pines wave with breezy murmuring!
    So sweet thy song, whose music might succeed
    To the wild melodies of Pan's own reed.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, William Lisle Bowles delivers a powerful performance in "Pictures From Theocritus - From Idyl I."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
